name: Downy mildew
regulatory_status: Routinely managed with copper compounds and synthetic fungicides; copper use capped at 28 kg/ha over 7 years under EU Reg 2018/1981
notes: Most economically damaging fungal disease in wet years across all Hungarian wine districts. Endemic in Europe since 1878.

name: Powdery mildew
regulatory_status: Managed with sulphur and DMI/SDHI fungicides; no specific HU restriction beyond EU MRLs
notes: Endemic across all Hungarian districts; severity rises in warm dry summers. Cleistothecia overwintering documented in HU vine regions (Acta Phytopathologica et Entomologica Hungarica 2007).

name: Grey mould / Botrytis bunch rot
regulatory_status: Standard EU fungicide approvals apply; in Tokaj, noble rot form is desired for aszú wines
notes: Dual role: destructive bunch rot in most regions; essential for Tokaji aszú in Tokaji district under humid autumns.

name: Black rot
regulatory_status: Managed with broad-spectrum fungicides
notes: Increasing concern in eastern Hungarian districts in wet years.

name: Crown gall
regulatory_status: No specific regulatory measure
notes: Often follows winter freeze damage.

name: Pierce's disease
regulatory_status: Xylella subject to EU emergency measures (Implementing Regulation 2020/1201); vector and pathogen not currently established in HU vineyards
notes: Future risk under climate warming; surveillance ongoing.

name: Grapevine leafroll disease
regulatory_status: Certified planting material required under EU Directive 2005/43/EC
notes: Widespread but chronic; not a 2025 emergency.

name: Grapevine fanleaf degeneration
regulatory_status: Nematode vector (Xiphinema index) management; certified material
notes: Soil-borne; affects replant sites.

name: Bois noir
regulatory_status: Not a quarantine pest in EU; managed via vector and weed host control
notes: Often confused with FD symptomatically; widespread in HU. Recovery in Kékfrankos documented (Eur. J. Plant Pathology 2019).

name: Flavescence dorée
regulatory_status: EU quarantine pest (Reg 2019/2072 Annex II); mandatory uprooting and vector control in demarcated zones
notes: See flavescence_doree_timeline.csv for full timeline.

name: American grapevine leafhopper
regulatory_status: Mandatory insecticide treatments in FD demarcated zones under NÉBIH decree
notes: Sole confirmed vector of FD phytoplasma. First HU record 2006 (EPPO Reporting Service Article 265).

name: European grapevine moth
regulatory_status: Pheromone monitoring widely used; mating disruption authorised
notes: Standard pest across all Hungarian districts.

name: Spotted wing drosophila
regulatory_status: No specific HU regulation
notes: First HU record 2012 near Táska (Somogy county) at a highway rest area; subsequently widespread. Increasing problem on thin-skinned grape varieties.

name: Brown marmorated stink bug
regulatory_status: No specific HU regulation
notes: First HU record 2013 in Budapest (Zootaxa 3780.1.8, 2014). Now widespread; emerging threat to fruit and wine.

name: Severe drought
regulatory_status: Emergency irrigation derogations in some districts
notes: 2022 was one of the driest summers on record across Central Europe. Hotspot of EU-27 drought damage; cell development delayed in grapevine due to July-August rain deficit. EU-wide losses ~13 billion EUR across 6 crops (Pinke et al. 2024 EuroChoices).

name: Late spring frost
regulatory_status: Standard frost-damage compensation
notes: April 2017 frost across Europe caused ~3.3 billion EUR damage to fruit and wine growers (~600 million EUR insured). Hungary affected 19-20 April 2017 with extreme precipitation and wet snow event in northern HU and around Budapest.

name: Late spring frost
regulatory_status: Standard frost-damage compensation
notes: April 2020 frost across Central Europe affected several HU districts; precise borvidék damage list not curated.

name: Late spring frost
regulatory_status: Crop insurance partial compensation under HU agricultural risk management scheme
notes: April 2024 frost (notably 22-23 April cold snap) damaged budburst nationwide. Per HNT 2024 sectoral report and feol.hu coverage: Eger and Mátra hit first, then Villány, Kunság, Hajós-Baja, Csongrád and parts of Dunántúl. Frost-damaged vineyard area reached 5,000-6,000 ha (~10% of HU vineyard area); local damage up to 80% in Alföld and Dunántúl pockets. ~3,000 ha damaged in Kunság/Hajós-Baja/Csongrád combined. 2024 national harvest fell to 3.73 million q, the lowest in a decade.

name: Hailstorm
regulatory_status: Insurance-based compensation; protected by Országos Jégkármérséklő Rendszer (national hail-mitigation generator system)
notes: Per NAK Országos Jégkármérséklő Rendszer reports: 2023 had an unusually active convective season; generators were operated on 96 days (15 April - 30 September), 176,639 generator-hours total. Reported national hail damage 30,678 ha (all crops, not vineyard-only) vs. ~72,000 ha in 2017 before the system was operational. Notable vineyard hail strikes: NW shore of Lake Balaton in late August 2023, and Mád (Tokaji) in July 2023. Per-borvidék vineyard-only breakdown is not published in primary sources.

name: Copper fungicide cap
regulatory_status: EU Reg 2018/1981 (13 December 2018) re-approved copper compounds and capped use on grapes at 28 kg Cu/ha over 7 years (~4 kg/ha/year average); approval valid Jan 2019 - 31 December 2025; copper classified as candidate for substitution
notes: Major constraint on organic viticulture against downy mildew. Renewal review pending.

name: Mancozeb withdrawal
regulatory_status: EU Reg 2020/2087 (14 December 2020) non-renewal; MS withdrew authorisations by 4 July 2021; final grace period expired 4 January 2022
notes: Removed a major contact fungicide from the downy mildew toolbox.

name: Glyphosate re-approval
regulatory_status: EU re-approved glyphosate for 10 years in November 2023 (Reg 2023/2660); approval valid until 15 December 2033. First EU approval was 1 July 2002; first renewal December 2017.
notes: Continues to be available for vineyard weed control.

name: Neonicotinoid outdoor ban
regulatory_status: EU outdoor use ban on three neonicotinoids (Reg 2018/783, 784, 785). Acetamiprid not banned; renewed until 28 February 2033 (Reg 2018/113).
notes: Limits options for leafhopper vector control - relevant to FD strategy. Acetamiprid remains the only outdoor-allowed neonicotinoid in EU.

name: Thiacloprid non-renewal
regulatory_status: EU Reg 2020/23 non-renewal; approval expired 30 April 2020; withdrawn 3 February 2020
notes: Non-renewal due to groundwater contamination and reproductive toxicity (EFSA 2019).

name: Chlorpyrifos non-renewal
regulatory_status: EU Reg 2020/18 (10 January 2020) non-renewal; MS withdrew authorisations by 16 February 2020; grace period expired 16 April 2020
notes: Removed organophosphate option for vine pests.

name: Dimethoate non-renewal
regulatory_status: EU Reg 2019/1090 (26 June 2019) non-renewal
notes: Previously used against Scaphoideus titanus.
